heading: Annual report on violations of law or executive order
label: 3110 Annual report on violations of law or executive order
notes: Construction, , , provided that:Pub. L. 113–293, title III, § 323(e)128 Stat. 4004Dec. 19, 2014“Nothing in this section [enacting this section and provisions set out as notes under this section] or the amendments made by this section shall be construed to alter any requirement existing on the date of the enactment of this Act [] to submit a report under any provision of law.”Dec. 19, 2014

Initial Report, , , provided that:Pub. L. 113–293, title III, § 323(b)128 Stat. 4004Dec. 19, 2014“The first report required under section 511 of the National Security Act of 1947 [], as added by subsection (a), shall be submitted not later than one year after the date of the enactment of this Act [].”50 U.S.C. 3110Dec. 19, 2014

Guidelines, , , provided that:Pub. L. 113–293, title III, § 323(c)128 Stat. 4004Dec. 19, 2014“Not later than 180 days after the date of the enactment of this Act [], the Director of National Intelligence, in consultation with the head of each element of the intelligence community, shall—Dec. 19, 2014“(1)issue guidelines to carry out section 511 of the National Security Act of 1947 [], as added by subsection (a); and50 U.S.C. 3110“(2)submit such guidelines to the congressional intelligence committees.”[For definitions of terms used in , set out above, see , set out as a note under .]section 323(c) of Pub. L. 113–293section 2 of Pub. L. 113–293section 3003 of this title

children.heading: Annual reports required
children.label: a Annual reports required
children.text: The Director of National Intelligence shall annually submit to the congressional intelligence committees a report on violations of law or executive order relating to intelligence activities by personnel of an element of the intelligence community that were identified during the previous calendar year.
determined by the director, head, or general counsel of any element of the intelligence community to have occurred;
referred to the Department of Justice for possible criminal prosecution; or
substantiated by the inspector general of any element of the intelligence community.
